name: Bell's Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: The light-cone causal structure and the experimental apparatus that isolates spacelike separated measurement events from external interference.

VINIT.justification: The uninitialized state before the entangled pair is generated; no correlations exist yet.

TANCH.justification: The sealed apparatus and light-cone structure that contains the measurement process and prevents external information leakage.

AFWD.justification: Directed transformation of the entangled state from the source toward the detectors.

AREV.justification: Reverse transformation where measurement outcomes retroactively fix the state at the source.

CLINK.justification: Sequential composition of measurement settings and their corresponding spin results.

IMSCRIB.justification: The quantum state recognizes its own measurement context; the observer becomes part of the entangled system.

FSPLIT.justification: The singlet state branches into two distinct, spacelike separated paths (Alice and Bob).

FFUSE.justification: Measurement outcomes from both branches are fused to compute the joint correlation, recovering the original singlet's predictive structure.

EVALT.justification: Affirmative evaluation: CHSH parameter exceeds classical bound (S > 2), confirming non-locality.

EVALF.justification: Negative evaluation: Local hidden variable theory predicts S ≤ 2, which is violated.

ENGAGR.justification: Both true and false simultaneously: local realism and quantum mechanics are held without resolution until measurement collapses the paradice.

IFIX.justification: Permanent, append-only fixation of the verified Bell inequality violation in the data log.

phase_3.void_description: Unmeasured potentiality; no definite spin values assigned, only probabilistic amplitudes.
phase_3.true_description: Quantum non-locality confirmed; measurement outcomes exhibit correlations exceeding classical limits.
phase_3.false_description: Local realism holds; outcomes are pre-determined by hidden variables within classical bounds.
phase_3.both_description: The EPR state; particles possess definite but unknown properties (local) while simultaneously exhibiting instantaneous correlation (non-local), held as a dialetheia.

steps.domain_action: Initialize the pre-entangled vacuum before any correlation exists.
Propagate the singlet state from the source toward the measurement apparatus.
Differentiate the singlet into two spacelike separated branches (Alice and Bob).
Forward morphism along Alice's branch toward her measurement setting.
Forward morphism along Bob's branch toward his measurement setting.
Branch Alice's path into measurement outcome possibilities (spin up/down).
Branch Bob's path into measurement outcome possibilities (spin up/down).
Hold the superposition of all four outcome pairs without collapse — the paradice of local hidden variables vs quantum entanglement.
Evaluate the classical local hidden variable bound (Bell inequality ≤ 2).
Evaluate the quantum mechanical prediction (CHSH parameter > 2).
Chain Alice's setting choice with her measurement outcome.
Chain Bob's setting choice with his measurement outcome.
Compose the joint probability distribution of outcomes.
Fuse Alice's and Bob's measurement outcomes to compute the correlation coefficient.
Self-reference the quantum state — the measurement apparatus becomes entangled with the system.
Back-propagate the collapse condition from the fused correlation to the source.
Record the verified violation of the Bell inequality as permanent experimental data.
Seal the experimental boundary — the closed system of measurement and result.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Quantum state tomography software that translates raw detector clicks into density matrices.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Classical communication channels between Alice and Bob for correlating measurement settings and outcomes.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: Quantum memory registers storing the entangled state's phase and amplitude information.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: Random number generators that independently and rapidly switch measurement bases to ensure spacelike separation.
phase_5.alfs_store: The CHSH inequality formulation and the Tsirelson bound as the canonical reference for non-locality verification.

phase_6.cycle_cost: Information gain from measurement outcomes; no energy is consumed in the correlation itself.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: Superposition of entangled states with maximal uncertainty about individual spins.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Definite correlated outcomes with reduced joint uncertainty but preserved total information.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The measurement extracts pre-existing correlation structure without erasing information; the round-trip preserves the singlet's informational content.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → AFWD → FSPLIT → AFWD → AFWD → FSPLIT → FSPLIT → ENGAGR → EVALF → EVALT → CLINK → CLINK → CLINK → FFUSE → IMSCRIB → AREV → IFIX → TANCH
-- Class: Bell's Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(10,4,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=18
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(6, 13)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑾  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑼 → 𐑚  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [2] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑾 → 𐑾  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [3] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑾  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [4] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑾 → 𐑚  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [5] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑾 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [6] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [7] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑚 → 𐑙  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [8] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [9] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [10] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑚 → 𐑙  | composition — regime coherence
--   [11] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑚 → 𐑙  | composition — regime coherence
--   [12] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑚 → 𐑙  | composition — regime coherence
--   [13] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑠  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [14] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑙 → 𐑗  | identity — self-imscription
--   [15] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑠 → 𐑭  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [16] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑗 → 𐑡  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [17] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑭 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def bells_theorem_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Initialize the pre-entangled vacuum before any correlation exists.)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑼 𐑚)  -- [1] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Propagate the singlet state from the source toward the measurement apparatus.)
  (.arrow 𐑚 𐑾 𐑾)  -- [2] FSPLIT | gran := 𐑚 | split δ — range decomposition (Differentiate the singlet into two spacelike separated branches (Alice and Bob).)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑾)  -- [3] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Forward morphism along Alice's branch toward her measurement setting.)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑾 𐑚)  -- [4] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Forward morphism along Bob's branch toward his measurement setting.)
  (.arrow 𐑚 𐑾 𐑚)  -- [5] FSPLIT | gran := 𐑚 | split δ — range decomposition (Branch Alice's path into measurement outcome possibilities (spin up/down).)
  -- FSPLIT [6] (gran := 𐑚) (Branch Bob's path into measurement outcome possibilities (spin up/down).) / FFUSE [13] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (4 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [9] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (Evaluate the quantum mechanical prediction (CHSH parameter > 2).)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [10] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Chain Alice's setting choice with her measurement outcome.)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [11] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Chain Bob's setting choice with his measurement outcome.)
        (.arrow 𐑱 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [12] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Compose the joint probability distribution of outcomes.)
      -- F-branch (2 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑳 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (Hold the superposition of all four outcome pairs without collapse — the parad...)
        (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (Evaluate the classical local hidden variable bound (Bell inequality ≤ 2).))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [13]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [13] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Fuse Alice's and Bob's measurement outcomes to compute the correlation coefficient.)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑙 𐑗)  -- [14] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Self-reference the quantum state — the measurement apparatus becomes entangle...)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑠 𐑭)  -- [15] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Back-propagate the collapse condition from the fused correlation to the source.)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑗 𐑡)  -- [16] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Record the verified violation of the Bell inequality as permanent experimenta...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑭 𐑼)  -- [17] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Seal the experimental boundary — the closed system of measurement and result.)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def bells_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (bells_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def bells_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (bells_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem bells_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
